content.problemStatement:
Chef has two arrays AAA and BBB of the same size NNN.

In one operation, Chef can:


Choose two integers iii and jjj (1≤i,j≤N)(1 \leq i, j \leq N)(1≤i,j≤N) and swap the elements AiA_iAi​ and BjB_jBj​.
Chef came up with a task to find the **minimum** possible value of **(Amax−AminA_{max} - A_{min}Amax​−Amin​)** after performing the swap operation any (possibly zero) number of times.

Since Chef is busy, can you help him solve this task?

Note that AmaxA_{max}Amax​ and AminA_{min}Amin​ denote the maximum and minimum elements of the array AAA respectively.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains one integer NNN — the number of elements in each array.
The second line consists of NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2,\ldots ,A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​ denoting the elements of the array AAA.
The third line consists of NNN space-separated integers B1,B2,…,BNB_1, B_2, \ldots ,B_NB1​,B2​,…,BN​ denoting the elements of the array BBB.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the **minimum** possible value of **(Amax−AminA_{max} - A_{min}Amax​−Amin​)** in the array AAA after doing swap operation any number of times.
constraints: Sum of N over all test cases <= 200000
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case: first line contains integer N, the size of the arrays. The second line contains N space‑separated integers A1 … AN. The third line contains N space‑separated integers B1 … BN.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the minimum possible value of (Amax − Amin) after any number of allowed swaps.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For each test case combine the two arrays, sort, and slide a window of size N. The minimal differences are 0, 1 and 2 respectively.
Combined sorted array: [2,3,4,4,6,7,7,8,8,9]. The best window of length 5 is [6,7,7,8,8] giving difference 2.
Combined sorted: [1,2,2,2,3,3,4,6]. Best window of size 4 is [2,2,2,3] with difference 1.

solution.approach: All elements of A and B together form a multiset C of size 2N. By swapping any element of A with any element of B we can choose any N elements from C to become the final array A. Therefore the problem reduces to selecting N numbers from the sorted list C such that the difference between the largest and smallest among the chosen numbers is minimal. After sorting C, we slide a window of length N and compute C[i+N-1] - C[i] for all i from 0 to N. The minimum of these differences is the answer.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N log N) per test case for sorting, overall O((sum N) log (max N)).
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) to store the combined array.

code.Python3:
import sys

def solve():
    data = sys.stdin.buffer.read().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out = []
    for _ in range(T):
        N = int(next(it))
        C = [int(next(it)) for _ in range(N)]
        C += [int(next(it)) for _ in range(N)]
        C.sort()
        ans = 10**18
        for i in range(N + 1):
            diff = C[i + N - 1] - C[i]
            if diff < ans:
                ans = diff
        out.append(str(ans))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    solve()
